notes:
Had to manually force game to use Proton as there's probably a broken Linux build from the devs. Had to use "--skip-intro" launch option otherwise game crashes with error when launching.

There's no audio at all. Can't progress through story mode as the game won't play a cut scene or something. Played a 1v1 match on multiplayer against a bot just fine.
title: Wargroove

notes: The into doesn't work but we can disable it using the '--skip-intro' launch option
title: Wargroove

notes: USE 3.16-6 beta for the best sound performance, game will not launch UNLESS YOU USE --skip-intro launch option, also has input delay problems, on nvidia disabling composition pipeline helped slightly. Other than input delay and intro not working game runs perfect
title: Wargroove

notes: Needs to launch with --skip-intro flag otherwise the game will crash. Other than that, the game runs flawlessly. No other issues.
title: Wargroove

notes:
I had to perform these tweaks to start the game:
1/ after installation, force steam play in game's properties.
2/ update the launch options with "%command% --skip-intro" to skip the intro because it makes the game crash.
Other than that, the game runs smoothly (~30min play in campaign mode)
title: Wargroove

notes: Requires --skip-intro launch option. Ingame-rendered cursor has slight latency.
title: Wargroove

notes: Had to use the --skip-intro launch option. Works well enough after that. Only problem that remains is the in-game cursor lags behind.
title: Wargroove

notes: Running after several reinstalls, setting --skip-intro, forcing steam play via steam beta, proton 3.16-6, and finally it succesfully started after running 'steam' from command line. Some input lag with the cursor and settings like fullscreen not working out of the box but otherwise gold experience
title: Wargroove

notes: have to use "--skip-into" flag. Played 8 hours using a controller. no issues.
title: Wargroove

notes: Is listed as a Linux title, but comes with no linux executable so you have to enable proton in the game properties. I tried with the --skip-intro flag mentioned in other posts. When I start it, nothing shows up and it completely hangs my system. I can't even switch to another virtual terminal or close the program.
title: Wargroove

notes: With 'Launch Options' set to '--skip-intro', Wargroove shuts down almost immediately after starting, using Proton 3.16-7 Beta, 3.16-4, and 3.7-8 Beta. On Proton 3.7-8, it will install some MS VC dependencies, but then it crashes due to a MediaFoundation initialization exception.
title: Wargroove

notes: I just followed this post: https://www.reddit.com/r/wargroove/comments/am1wps/psa_as_of_now_wargroove_does_work_on_linux/ Just force use of Proton through the game's properties menu and add --skip-intro to launch options. Very easy to do.
title: Wargroove

notes: As other said, use --skip-intro, otherwise it'll crash. Also fullscreen not fully working (task bar visible) for me (might be my DE, KDE). I felt the game mouse a bit slow, but it might just be that way. No difference between standard and OpenGL version. Multiplayer not tested.
title: Wargroove

notes: System Freeze and must hard reboot after starting the game.

notes: works perfectly after setting it to run under proton via properties and adding --skip-intro to launch options

notes: Works fine after "--skip-intro" in launch options. Had to disable fullscreen and reenter my resolution to get fullscreen working.

notes: --skip-intro is needed to launch the game and you have to toggle fullscreen a couple of times to get it working

notes: Tried --skip intro with opengl start up and with the normal too and different proton versions but this game causes whole pc to freeze up. Have to force the pc to shut down.

title: Wargroove

notes: If you press the play button it gives you the following error message: https://imgur.com/gallery/QwjegCL, which states that wargroove is missing an executable (it appears to be a 32-bit executable, whose parent directory simply does not exist). I posted a thread on the wargroove forums and it doesn't seem like they are planning on doing anything about it as of now.
title: Wargroove

notes: Use --skip intro in launch options. Also force a compatability tool. Now like others said it will/might freeze. It froze for me, but after waiting like 2 Minutes the game starts and everything unfreezes. It basically freezes the time it would take for the intro.
title: Wargroove
